props.heading: Build Modern Websites with Composable Blocks
props.subheading: Ship fast with pre-built, testable components. No compromises on design or flexibility.
props.imageAlt: Browser mockup showing a block-based website with floating components

props.title: Why Block-Based Sites?
props.body:
Building websites from composable blocks gives you the **flexibility** of custom code with the **speed** of a page builder. Each block is self-contained, tested, and styled — so you can mix and match without breaking anything.

Here's what makes this approach stand out:

- **Consistent design** — every block shares the same design tokens, so your site looks cohesive without extra effort
- **Easy content updates** — edit a single JSON file to change text, images, or layout across any page
- **Static by default** — pages are pre-rendered at build time for instant load times and zero server costs
- **Fully extensible** — add new block types as simple React components with a Zod schema

Whether you're launching a marketing site, a documentation hub, or a portfolio — blocks let you ship fast and iterate faster. No CMS lock-in, no drag-and-drop compromises, just clean components and structured content.

items.q: How do I add new pages?
items.a: Add a new entry to content/pages.json with a slug, title, and array of blocks. The site will pick it up automatically on the next build.
Yes — the CSS custom properties in globals.css control colors, typography, spacing, and shapes across all blocks.

items.q: How do I deploy this site?
items.a: Run 'next build' to generate a fully static export in the 'out' directory. Upload it to any static hosting provider like Vercel, Netlify, or S3.

props.title: Ready to get started?
props.description: Start building your site with reusable block components today.

props.heading: Building with Blocks
props.subheading: The web is moving toward composable, block-based architecture — self-contained components with typed schemas, isolated styles, and structured content. In this article, we explore why this approach is winning over teams of every size, and how you can start building with blocks today.
props.imageAlt: Abstract illustration of colorful building blocks

props.body:
The web development landscape has shifted dramatically. Gone are the days of monolithic page templates and tightly-coupled CMS themes. In their place, a new paradigm has emerged: **block-based composition**.

The idea is simple but powerful — break every page into self-contained, reusable components (blocks), each with its own schema, styles, and rendering logic. A Hero block doesn't know or care what comes after it. A FAQ accordion works the same whether it's on a landing page or a support hub. This isolation is what makes the approach so robust.

What makes this more than just "components" is the **content layer**. Each block declares a typed schema — a contract for the data it needs. Content lives in structured JSON, completely separate from presentation. Change the text without touching code. Swap the layout without migrating content. Deploy to any static host with zero runtime dependencies.

props.quote: The best architectures are the ones that let you change your mind. Block-based sites give you that freedom — swap, reorder, and remix without fear of breaking anything.
props.author: Alex Chen

props.title: Why It Works
props.body:
The block-based approach succeeds because it aligns with how teams actually work. Designers think in sections. Content authors think in fields. Developers think in components. Blocks are the natural meeting point.

Here are the key benefits we've seen in practice:

- **Faster iteration** — add a new section by dropping a block into the page array, no template surgery required
- **Fewer regressions** — each block is isolated and tested independently, so changes to one never ripple into another
- **Better collaboration** — designers prototype with real blocks, content authors edit JSON directly, developers extend the system with new block types
- **Portable content** — your pages.json works with any renderer: React, Vue, server-side templates, or even native apps
- **Zero-config deployment** — static export means no servers, no databases, no cold starts

The composability doesn't stop at individual pages. Once you have a library of blocks, building a new page is more like curating a playlist than writing a novel. Pick the blocks you need, fill in the content, and ship.

props.title: Want to see it in action?
props.description: Head back to the home page to explore the full sample site built with blocks.
